## McDonald’s unveils massive global expansion

McDonald’s has unveiled an ambitious roadmap to expand its global footprint, with plans to launch 8,000 new restaurants by the end of 2026. This aggressive expansion is the first phase of a larger mission to operate at least 50,000 locations worldwide by 2027. The strategy focuses on capturing high-traffic areas and entering emerging markets while simultaneously modernizing existing sites to keep pace with new corporate standards. [...] GEEKSPIN

You are here:

# McDonald’s opening 8,000 restaurants with major upgrades

## The shake-up plan includes menu updates and expanded digital tools

by
Christian Saclao
January 21, 2026, 9:00 am

McDonald's opening 8,000 restaurants with major upgrades 1

McDonald’s is gearing up for one of the biggest transformations in its history — and it could change the way millions of customers experience the Golden Arches. The fast-food giant has announced plans to open 8,000 restaurants around the world by the end of 2026, while bringing major upgrades, refreshed menus, and more digital tools to its lineup. Keep reading to uncover all the changes on the horizon and how they might impact your next McDonald’s order.

## McDonald’s unveils massive global expansion [...] ## McDonald’s rolls out major menu and beverage overhaul

As part of this aggressive footprint expansion, McDonald’s is also reimagining its menu to capture a wider variety of dining habits. The most notable change comes from the company’s “CosMc’s” spinoff concept, which focuses on the high-margin beverage market. Following successful pilot testing, CosMc’s-inspired drinks are slated to roll out to more than 500 locations.

The innovation extends beyond the drink station and into the kitchen, where the chain is doubling down on its most popular proteins. McDonald’s is significantly expanding its chicken lineup, reintroducing a variety of wraps and launching upgraded sandwiches to meet the growing consumer demand for poultry.

private_opportunities.snippet:
# McDonald’s Plans to Open 8,000 Restaurants by End of 2027 and Shake Up Menu

Published

Jan 20, 2026 at 11:12 AM EST

updated

Jan 21, 2026 at 09:37 AM EST

Amanda Greenwood

Associate News Editor

McDonalds is planning to complete its global expansion of more than 8,000 new restaurants across 50,000 locations by 2027, and this year, also plans to change its pricing structure and menu offerings.

## What Changes Is McDonald’s Planning?

### Global Expansion

McDonald’s plans to reach 50,000 locations worldwide by the end of 2027, including the opening of more than 8,000 new restaurants (about 900 in the U.S.), an initiative that began in 2024. The expansion is designed to position the company for value‑focused consumers and to intensify competition in high‑growth international markets. [...] Correction 1/21/26, 9:36 a.m. ET: This article was corrected to state that McDonald's plans to complete its global expansion by the end of 2027 and to clarify its new pricing guidelines.

# McDonald’s CEO confirms 8,000 new stores opening in major expansion push

Plus, read up on how the chain grew into the global fast food superpower as we know it today

Maya Lanzone  , Consumer Reporter

Published:  09:09, 5 Nov 2025

|

Updated:  10:12, 5 Nov 2025

McDonald’s CEO confirms 8,000 new stores opening in major expansion push

 Click to share on Twitter (Opens in new window)
 Click to share on Facebook (Opens in new window)

THE top boss of McDonald’s has announced that the fast food giant has some major expansion plans in the works, on a mission to grow its global presence. [...] The chain’s CEO, Chris Kempczinski, shared McDonald’s plan to roll out over 8,000 new restaurants by the end of 2027.

### Sign up for the Money newsletter

### Thank you!

NINTCHDBPICT001036321476

McDonald’s is on a mission to expand globally as it races to regain its title of largest fast food chain in the worldCredit: Alamy

 BIZ-MCDONALDS-CEO-CHICAGO-TB

McDonald’s CEO Chris Kempczinski discussed the fast food giant’s expansion plans at a company earnings call on Wednesday morningCredit: Getty

Advertisement

McDonald’s, one of the most iconic fast food chains, has lost its crown as the largest quick service restaurant in the world to the Chinese company Mixue Bingcheng as of late 2024. [...] As the quick service chain continues to pursue its ambitious development plan, this means that the fast food giant is on track to open over 8,000 new locations around the world over the next two years.

McDonald’s announced in May that roughly 900 of these new stores would be located in the US, with the vast majority of the new restaurants slated for international markets.

China is a major expansion point for the chain, which is plotting to open thousands of new locations there in the coming years as McDonald’s competes against Mixue Bingcheng for consumers’ dollars.
